summary: Sprint 10 Super Admin Hub E2E frontend testing complete. All critical flows PASS: auth (demo tile + email/password + persistent Zustand + signout + unknown-email error), Overview (4 KPIs, MRR area chart, signups bar chart, top tenants sorted by MRR, 6 audit items, at-risk pills for himalaya-camps + the-mint-house), Tenants grid (10 rows page 1 of 12 with 'Page 1 of 2 · 12 tenants', search filters to 1 row for 'bhairav', empty state, all filter pills, row actions preview/impersonate/details), Tenant Detail (buttons + 404 slug), 4-step Provision wizard (profile→domain→brand→seed→launch → redirect to /tenants), Feature Flags (7 toggles + 7 expanders + 8 tenant override chips), Billing (paid/overdue + suspended the-mint-house shows overdue), Audit (6 entries), PMS + guest storefront isolation intact. No regressions detected.

issues: Recharts logs multiple 'width(-1) height(-1)' warnings on initial mount before ResponsiveContainer measures parent. Non-blocking; charts render correctly after layout settles. Consider setting minWidth=0 / a fixed aspect on ResponsiveContainer.

action_items: Nit: Review request document referenced data-testid names like 'kpi-active-tenants', 'kpi-mrr', 'kpi-avg-health', 'kpi-uptime'. Actual implementation uses 'super-kpi-active-tenants', 'super-kpi-mrr', 'super-kpi-avg.-health' (note the '.' from 'Avg.'), 'super-kpi-uptime-sla'. Either the doc or the test-ids should be aligned. Consider slugifying the KPI label with a helper that strips punctuation to avoid 'avg.-health'.
Nit: Recharts ResponsiveContainer warnings on first paint in SuperOverview — add minHeight/minWidth or fixed aspect to silence.
critical_code_review_comments: KPI data-testid uses raw label.toLowerCase().replace(/\s/g,'-') which produces 'super-kpi-avg.-health' (period retained). Sanitize with .replace(/[^a-z0-9-]/g,'') for cleaner ids.

SuperProvision profile step has 6 inputs but only Brand/Owner-Email/City are required; testing-agents may target inputs by index. Adding stable data-testids like 'provision-brand-name', 'provision-owner-email', 'provision-city' would improve testability.
test_credentials: platform@aurahotels.com / any password (demo tile also works)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reset localStorage key 'aura_super_admin_v1' between auth scenarios. Tenant slugs used in tests: bhairavgarh, himalaya-camps, the-mint-house, kalinga-court, coastline-6, sundara-spa (not 'front-desk-hub-4' — that is the deployment hostname, not a tenant slug). KPI test-ids on Overview are prefixed 'super-kpi-*' not 'kpi-*'. Provision wizard step 1 requires Brand Name / Owner Email / City to enable Continue.
